title: Saudi Arabia Trades Oil Barrels for Batteries
summary: As Saudi Arabia redirects more and more crude from the east to the west, where it can export it safely, oil seems to be all that everyone is watching. But not Saudi Arabia itself. In fact, the world’s second-largest oil producer and top exporter has big plans in another area: battery storage. Batte…

title: US Crude Oil Inventories See Surprise 10 Million Barrel Spike
summary: The American Petroleum Institute (API) estimated that crude oil inventories in the United States rose by a staggering 10.263 million barrels in the week ending March 27. In the week prior, US crude oil inventories rose by 2.3 million barrels. Analysts had expected a draw of 1.3 million barrels in t…

title: Guyana's Oil Boom Will Boost Energy Security in the Americas
summary: Guyana, once among South America's most impoverished nations, is experiencing an epic oil boom. In a mere four years, the country of less than one million went from first discovery to first oil and is now lifting over 900,000 barrels per day. There are signs of significant production growth ahead. …

title: BP loses head of EV charging as it accelerates pivot back to oil and gas
summary: Martin Thomsen’s departure comes ahead of arrival of oil major’s new chief executive this week
